reference_audit.notes: Current tests carry only the imported RFC 9001 IDs in scope; no stale requirement refs were found under src/Incursa.Quic. The new requirement-home tests for S5-0001 and S5-0002 are now part of the audit surface.

requirements.title: Interpret uppercase BCP 14 keywords
requirements.remaining_gap: Document-level BCP 14 interpretation rule; no live code or test artifact is expected in the current repository.
requirements.notes: This is a spec-governance rule rather than an executable behavior. Keep it document-only unless the repository defines a canonical artifact for it.

requirements.title: Assume packet confidentiality
requirements.remaining_gap: The repository still lacks the TLS handshake, packet-protection, and QUIC/TLS delivery surface needed to satisfy this clause.
requirements.notes: Leave this untouched until the TLS handshake and packet-protection subsystems exist.

requirements.title: Send data as QUIC frames
requirements.notes: Added a public STREAM-frame formatter, updated the public API manifest, extended positive/negative round-trip tests, and added a dry benchmark lane for STREAM formatting.

requirements.title: Carry handshake data in CRYPTO frames
requirements.notes: Existing CRYPTO-frame parse/format coverage already satisfies the handshake-data carriage clause; this pass retained the RFC 9001 trace tags on the live tests.

requirements.title: Define CRYPTO frame boundaries
requirements.notes: The CRYPTO formatter now rejects frames whose offset-plus-length would exceed the QUIC stream ceiling, matching the parser-side boundary check.

requirements.title: Package and encrypt CRYPTO frames
requirements.remaining_gap: The repository still lacks the handshake transport and encryption-level plumbing needed to package CRYPTO frames end-to-end.
requirements.notes: These clauses depend on a full CRYPTO-frame transport path and encryption-level scheduling.

requirements.title: Protect packets with TLS-derived keys
requirements.remaining_gap: The non-Initial material boundary exists, but packet protect/open helpers and handshake packet I/O are still absent.
requirements.notes: Introduced a narrow internal packet-protection material model and rejected malformed Initial-level inputs.

requirements.title: Use the TLS-negotiated AEAD
requirements.remaining_gap: Negotiated AEAD-bound material is now threaded through the bridge/runtime seam, but packet protect/open helpers are still absent.
requirements.notes: Added explicit AEAD binding, supported-algorithm validation, and runtime-seam storage for negotiated packet-protection material.

requirements.title: Leave Version Negotiation packets unprotected
requirements.notes: Version Negotiation packets remain unprotected by construction; the response formatter and fuzz coverage now carry the RFC 9001 trace tag.

requirements.title: Protect Retry packets with AEAD_AES_128_GCM
requirements.remaining_gap: The repository still lacks the packet-protection and key-derivation layer needed to encrypt or protect these packet types.
requirements.notes: These clauses depend on packet-protection, key derivation, and encryption-aware packet assembly.

benchmark_run.summary: 3 benchmarks executed successfully in Dry mode

notes: The audit found no stale or wrong requirement IDs in the selected RFC 9001 scope.

The completed items in scope are REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0012, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0003; REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0002 are now partial.
All remaining requirements in scope are explicitly deferred, partial, or blocked with concrete dependency notes.
